mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the national unemployment rate drops below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The model produces negative or zero predictive power when UNRATE crosses below 5.0%, or forward returns in this regime do not exceed the unconditional mean by at least 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and rising residential mortgage debt indicates a synchronized credit and construction boom that expands aggregate demand, lifting equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when both HOUST and RSAFS rise concurrently, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansion phases, a sustained rise in the industrial production index signals peak capacity utilization and pricing power, which temporarily elevates corporate margins and drives short-term equity outperformance.
failure_condition: Industrial production rises while S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of observed instances, indicating a decoupling of supply strength from equity pricing.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real gross domestic product confirms underlying economic momentum, which reduces earnings recession risk and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Real GDP growth remains positive while 20-day forward equity returns are negative in over half of the sample periods, invalidating the growth-to-equity transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: CPI prints above the 0.4 percent threshold while S&P 500 20-day forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of cases, or the predictive rank correlation stays below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent rises in total nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate validate a self-reinforcing labor market recovery, which sustains consumer demand and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising payrolls and falling unemployment fails to generate positive 60-day equity returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient in a panel regression is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery environment, an industrial production index exceeding 100 combined with accelerating real GDP growth confirms a broad-based demand-supply rebalancing, which rapidly lifts equity risk premiums and drives short-term positive returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal of INDPRO above 100 and rising GDPC1 produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of observations, or the joint predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS.
